s50442068.txt.findings:
FINDINGS:  PA and lateral views of the chest were obtained.  Lungs are
hyperinflated with flattened diaphragms and coarsened reticular markings
compatible with emphysema/COPD.  There is no focal consolidation, pleural
effusion or pneumothorax.  There is prominence of the pulmonary hila, right
greater than left, likely reflective of pulmonary hypertension. Heart size is
top normal.  Bony structures appear intact with dextroscoliosis of the
T-spine.
s50442068.txt.impression:
IMPRESSION:  Mild cardiomegaly with prominence of central pulmonary arteries,
likely indicates pulmonary arterial hypertension given the underlying
emphysema.  Please correlate clinically.  No evidence of pneumonia.

s56181233.txt.findings:
FINDINGS:  AP and lateral views of the chest are obtained.  Since the prior
study, there has been much improvement in the bilateral lung base
consolidations likely representing atelectasis.  There has also been
improvement in the previously seen bilateral pleural effusions.  There is no
evidence of congestive heart failure or prominent pulmonary edema.  There is
no new focal consolidation or evidence of pneumothorax.  The previously seen
left subclavian line remains in standard position, terminating in the upper
SVC.

CONCLUSION:  Much improved bilateral lung base atelectasis and bilateral
pleural effusions.  No new focal consolidation, pulmonary edema or
pneumothorax.

s52496484.txt.findings:
FINDINGS:

PA and lateral chest radiographs were obtained.  The lungs are well expanded
and clear.  There is no focal scarring in the right upper lobe is unchanged.
Posterior rib deformities are stable.  There is no consolidation effusion or
pneumothorax.  Cardiac and mediastinal contours are normal.  There is no
pneumoperitoneum.
s52496484.txt.impression:
IMPRESSION:

No acute cardiopulmonary process.  No free air.

s58383244.txt.findings:
FINDINGS:

The heart is borderline enlarged, in the mediastinal and hilar contours are
normal. There is no pleural effusion or pneumothorax.  The lungs are not
hyperinflated, and there is no focal consolidation concerning for pneumonia. A
right chest port is present with tip terminating near the cavoatrial junction.
The upper abdomen is unremarkable in appearance.
s58383244.txt.impression:
IMPRESSION:

No pneumonia. Normal lung volumes. Borderline cardiomegaly.

s51474316.txt.impression:
IMPRESSION:

Previously seen mild mediastinal prominence has resolved.  There is minimal
pulmonary edema and there are small bilateral effusions. There are no
pneumothoraces.

s54551565.txt.findings:
FINDINGS:

PA and lateral views of the chest provided. There is no focal consolidation,
effusion, or pneumothorax. The cardiomediastinal silhouette is normal. Imaged
osseous structures are intact.  No free air below the right hemidiaphragm is
seen.

s59724216.txt.impression:
IMPRESSION:

In comparison with the study of ___, there is no evidence of acute
cardiopulmonary disease. No pneumonia, vascular congestion, pleural effusion,
or evidence for pulmonary or skeletal metastases.

s50915465.txt.findings:
FINDINGS:

As compared to the prior examination, loculated right effusion with adjacent
subpleural atelectasis has slightly increased since the prior.  Most notably

stable.  No pulmonary edema.  Cardiomediastinal contours are unchanged.  No
pneumothorax.  Left-sided rib changes also unchanged.
s50915465.txt.impression:

Slight increase in the right loculated effusion and adjacent
atelectasis/opacity.

s51918830.txt.findings:
FINDINGS:

The lungs are clear without focal consolidation.  No pleural effusion or
pneumothorax is seen.  The cardiac silhouette remains top normal.  Mediastinal
contours are stable.  The hilar contours are stable.  Likely osteophytes are
seen at various levels along the thoracic spine.
